thesis: FuelCell Energy is a speculative fuel cell power infrastructure company with a $1.14B backlog and a pivot toward data center baseload power amid surging AI-driven electricity demand. Q2 FY2026 revenue of $35.6M and persistent net losses reflect a pre-profitability company trading on optionality. The 15-year weighted average generation backlog provides visibility, but backlog decline (~10% from peak) and technology risk create uncertainty. SPEC. BUY for thematic exposure to Grid & Power within AI Buildout, with tight position sizing.

drivers: Data center contracts accelerate, validating the pivot to AI baseload power
SOFC commercialization opens higher-efficiency, lower-maintenance technology
Backlog stabilizes and expands on new generation and service contracts
Path to profitability emerges via generation margin improvement and scale

risks: Cash burn continues and forces dilutive secondary offering
Backlog erosion accelerates as customers delay or cancel contracts
Technology competition from SOFC, PEM, or grid-scale batteries wins data center market
Execution failure on data center pivot leaves company without growth catalyst

base_case.assumptions: Backlog stabilizes near $1.1B, revenue grows modestly to $130M+, gross margin approaches breakeven by FY2027, and data center pipeline generates at least one meaningful contract. No major dilution event.

catalysts.event: Q3 FY2026 Earnings — test of backlog stabilization and revenue trajectory
Potential data center power contract announcements
Q4 FY2026 Earnings and FY2027 guidance preview
SOFC development milestone or partnership update

key_risks: Persistent net losses and unclear path to profitability
Backlog decline (~10% from peak) eroding revenue visibility
Technology risk — MCFC vs SOFC vs competing fuel cell and battery technologies
Customer concentration (Goonga Green Energy significant in Q1)
Dilution risk from history of secondary offerings
Execution risk on data center pivot against Bloom Energy and traditional power
